TEK.description: Teak is a large tropical hardwood tree prized for its durable, water-resistant timber. Native to South and Southeast Asia, it is one of the most commercially valuable timber species in the world.
TEK.carbon_note: High carbon sequestration due to large biomass accumulation. A single mature teak sequesters approximately 48 kg CO₂ per year.

NEM.description: Neem is a fast-growing evergreen tree native to the Indian subcontinent. It is widely used in traditional medicine, agriculture, and agroforestry systems across India.
NEM.carbon_note: Medium carbon sequestration. Neem's rapid growth partly offsets its moderate biomass. Widely planted in India for ecological restoration.

MNG.description: The mango tree is a tropical fruit tree that originated in South Asia and has been cultivated for thousands of years. It is the national tree of Bangladesh and plays a critical role in agroforestry.
MNG.carbon_note: Medium sequestration. Mango trees in agroforestry settings provide dual benefits — fruit production and carbon storage over their long lifespan.

PEP.description: The Peepal tree holds deep cultural and religious significance in India and Southeast Asia. It is one of the few trees that produces oxygen 24 hours a day and supports rich biodiversity.
PEP.carbon_note: High sequestration, aided by its exceptional longevity and large canopy. Sacred trees are rarely felled, making them effective long-term carbon stores.

BAM.description: Bamboo is technically a grass, but functions ecologically like a tree. It is the fastest-growing woody plant on Earth and has remarkable carbon sequestration potential per hectare.
BAM.carbon_note: Very high CO₂ uptake rate due to extremely rapid growth. One of the most cost-effective carbon sequestration options for tropical and subtropical regions.

MGR.description: Red Mangrove is a coastal tree that grows in saltwater and brackish environments. Its distinctive prop roots create critical habitat for marine life and protect coastlines from erosion.
MGR.carbon_note: The highest CO₂ sequestration in this dataset — mangroves store carbon both in biomass and in waterlogged soil (blue carbon), making them extremely valuable ecosystems.

RSW.description: Indian Rosewood is a large deciduous tree prized for its fine-grained, fragrant timber. It is a protected species under CITES and is critically important for tropical forest carbon stocks.
RSW.carbon_note: High sequestration due to very large trunk diameter and biomass. Though slow-growing, long-lived rosewood trees are among the most significant individual carbon stores.

MOR.description: Moringa is a fast-growing, drought-resistant tree native to India. Often called the 'miracle tree', it provides highly nutritious leaves, pods, and seeds and is used extensively in food security programs.
MOR.carbon_note: Low carbon sequestration per tree due to small size and thin trunk, but very fast growth cycles and multipurpose use make it valuable in agroforestry systems.
